If anything you want to make the other things more attractive and ultimately solve their goal. Alright, what should the cancellation policy be? Start whenever you want and that and the money that you put down will apply. If they like, no matter what, if someone really keeps blocking at you, give them the money back. Alright, that being said, what we gave was a service based guarantee, not a satisfaction guarantee. And there's a difference. A service based guarantees that if you do X, Y and Z, I'll continue to service you for free past X point satisfaction guarantees that no matter what, for whatever reason, you can cancel and give your money back. Those are very different guarantees. The service guarantee, in my opinion, tends to be stronger and expose you to less risk. Alright, should we stop doing six week 42 day challenge offers? No, absolutely not. You totally do those. You sell this to these people who walk in the door. Like, I think I actually have a beautiful little, let's see here. Here's Mel. She actually, she's a four for five. Her average upfront ticket is $2,700. And I said, are you running 20 days, six week? And she said I'm running 42 days. Alright, so yes, you can run 42 day offers, six week offers, you'll notice that your average ticket, if you're running a non free offer will be twice as high usually as a free offer, but you'll make up for the free on volume. So it's really just a question of what you feel like dealing with. You're going to get higher shows on average, you'll get more volume and you'll get a higher average ticket, but you'll get more people with a free offer. So it's kind of up to you whether you want to grow your teeth faster, you want to have more of our cash, it's up to you. Okay. Where would six week challenge go on the diagnostic pricing sheet?
